title: heloniopsis breviscapa var. yakusimensis
intro: Heloniopsis breviscapa var. yakusimensis, also known as Yakushima golden bellflower or Yakushima bellflower, is a rare and endangered plant species native to Yakushima Island in Japan. It belongs to the family Melanthiaceae and is known for its unique golden bell-shaped flowers and medicinal properties. The plant contains breviscapide, a flavonoid glycoside with anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties, which can help reduce blood pressure and prevent cardiovascular diseases. In traditional Chinese medicine, Heloniopsis breviscapa var. yakusimensis is prepared into herbal supplements or used as a tea to promote cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and improve blood circulation.
